subject: Project Management
title: California High-Speed Rail
description: A quick, practical guide to California High-Speed Rail.

hook.text: In 2008, California voters approved $10 billion for a bullet train from LA to San Francisco.

buildup.text: The original plan: 2 hours 40 minutes, $33 billion budget, completed by 2020.

discovery.text: By 2024, the cost had ballooned to over $100 billion. Not a single mile of track was operational.

twist.text: The project was scaled back to a 171-mile segment in the Central Valley — nowhere near LA or SF.

climax.text: California's bullet train became America's most expensive lesson in mega-project planning failure.

punchline.text: A hundred billion dollars and counting. Zero passengers.

quiz.question: What happened to California's high-speed rail budget?
options: It ballooned from $33 billion to over $100 billion
It came in under the original estimate
It was cancelled after the first year
